Institute in charge : OEC
The vessels less than 7 m long are excluded from this sampling scheme (not less than 8m)

Institute in charge : OEC
The two sampling schemes (DACOR and CF-DCF) follow the same basic protocol for monitoring small-scale coastal fisheries in Corsica. From 2022 (NWP 2022-2024), CF-DCF monitoring has been expanded to include sampling vessels that are too small (less than 7m long) for on-board monitoring through shore-based monitoring.

Institute in charge : Ifremer
Vessels under 5m are very unlikely to be sampled and vessels over 50m are not covered by ObsMer but by self-sampling. 
Difficult to grade the importance relative to other fisheries, because ObsMer  focuses on almost every fisheries in mainland France (except Corsica - see FRA_CF-DCF).

ExclusionCriteria: Small vessel size~Vessel space limitation~Low fishing effort
EndUser: Dedicated projects use~EU Commission~Fishing industry~ICES WGBYC~National use~Academia
Comments:
Institute in charge : OFB
OBSCAMe is a pilot project aiming to test implementation of cameras on board vessels to observe cetaceans bycatch. It was realized on 5 followed by 20 voluntary vessels. 
Other protected species are observed (birds, elasmobranchs, turtles and amphihalin fishes) but this data is not shared actually with end-users. The vessels range in size from 7.70 to 27.50 meters.

ExclusionCriteria: Small vessel size~Vessel space limitation~Low fishing effort
EndUser: Dedicated projects use~EU Commission~Fishing industry~ICES WGBYC~National use
Comments:
Institute in charge : OFB
OBSCAME+ is the continuation of the OBSCAMe project. Unlike OBSCAMe, vessels were designated and participation is mandatory. Other protected species are observed (birds, elasmobranchs, turtles and amphihalin fishes), this data is collected voluntarily on part of vessels as for now (Equipment phase in progress). The vessels range in size from 7.70 to 27.50 meters.

EndUser: Academia~Accreditation schemes~Dedicated projects use~EU Commission~Fishing industry~GFCM~ICES WGBYC~MSFD~National use~NGO's~OSPAR
Comments:
Institute in charge : OFB
Sampling project dedicated to reduce the main causes of mortality for 23 protected or vulnerable species along metropolitan coastlines: mortality in fishing gear, sources of disturbance, and loss and degradation of functional habitats. With regards to reduction of accidental bycatch, devices or alternative fishing practices will be tested, from 2025 to 2027, on-board approximately 70 fishing vessels.

Comments:
Institute in charge : MNHN
Prior to 2017, no selection in auction was made. 
Since 2018, the survey occurs in a selection of 10 auctions. Selection is based on the total landings of elasmobranch in the auctionx as well as allowing a coherent geographical coverage of the fleet along the French coast from the middle of the Bay pf Biscay to the Eastern Channel.
